subjective_review::.::holistic_unreviewe.summary: No holistic codebase review on record — run `desloppify review --prepare`

resolution_attestation.text: Holistic review refreshed; coverage marker superseded

review::.::holistic::cross_module_archit.summary: Contract conformance logic is split across conformance and feature tests, creating boundary drift risk.

evidence: `fixtures_test.go` validates fixture↔binding parity (`TestActionContractFixturesMatchCanonicalBindings`, lines 31-65) and upload payload-rule presence (`TestUploadActionBindingSpecsExposePayloadRules`, lines 88-109).

`actions_test.go` independently validates upload action↔contract parity (`TestUploadParserActionsMatchUploadContractRegistry`, lines 38-79) and payload-rule behavior matrix (`TestUploadActionBindingsValidateRuntimePayloadRules`, lines 96-155).
Both tests derive expectations from `contracts.UploadActionBindingSpecs()` but keep separate assertion logic, so policy changes require synchronized edits in two packages.
detail.suggestion: Consolidate contract-governance assertions under `protocol/contracts/conformance` as reusable helpers (or a shared conformance matrix), then have `uploadsvc` keep only behavior-specific integration checks. This preserves a single architectural boundary for protocol-policy verification.

review::.::holistic::cross_module_archit.summary: Upload action policy is duplicated across catalog constants, docs, and tests instead of one executable boundary.

evidence: `catalog/action/specs.go` defines payload policy in data (`RequiredPayloads`/`ForbiddenPayloads`, lines 72-85).
`uploadsvc/doc.go` repeats the same policy as prose (`reset must never carry open-endpoint`, lines 4-7).
`uploadsvc/actions_test.go` re-encodes forbidden/required payload semantics with explicit error expectations (lines 114-151), creating another independent expression of the same rules.
detail.suggestion: Make the contract catalog the sole policy source and drive docs/tests from it: add a small generator/helper that renders rule text and table-driven test cases from `UploadActionBindingSpecs`, so policy updates flow through one boundary.

review::.::holistic::abstraction_fitness.summary: Wide callback-bag runtimes create low-leverage indirection across orchestration paths

evidence: `importerRuntime` embeds three runtime structs with many callbacks and requires multiple requirement-specific validators (`validateImporter*Runtime`).
`workflowRuntime` embeds multiple orchestration op structs plus custom callbacks, then repeatedly resolves through `seams.ResolveRuntime`.
`runtime_ops.go` defines many granular `*Ops` structs that mainly forward existing package functions, then assembles them into a registry bundle.
`startup_orchestrator.go` defines multiple runtime structs for init/background/runtime phases with callback fields and group-tag validation.
detail.suggestion: Collapse function-bag structs into a smaller set of capability interfaces per workflow (for example `ImportProvisioner`, `TransitionPublisher`, `ContainerController`) and inject concrete services once at orchestration boundaries instead of passing merged callback structs through inner helpers.

review::.::holistic::abstraction_fitness.summary: Background-service abstraction is triplicated across spec list, callback map, and setter switch

evidence: Service identity is maintained in `startBackgroundServiceName` constants and again in `startBackgroundServiceSpecs`.
`serviceCallbacks()` separately maps each constant to runtime fields.
`setServiceCallback()` repeats the same mapping as a long `switch`, requiring synchronized edits for every new service and creating drift-prone abstraction bookkeeping.
detail.suggestion: Introduce a single canonical service descriptor table containing name, fail-fast policy, WG requirement, and getter/setter adapters for callback fields; generate specs and callback binding from that table to remove manual triplication.

review::.::holistic::abstraction_fitness.summary: Runtime resolution is repeated inside inner helpers instead of once per request/workflow

evidence: Importer helpers (`finalizeUploadOnCompletion`, `configureUploadedPier`, `runImportedPierWorkflow`, `allChunksReceived`) each resolve runtime independently via variadic runtime parameters.
Workflow helpers (`publishTransition`, `emitWorkflowTransition`, `persistShipConf`, `urbitCleanDelete`, `areSubdomainsAliases`) repeatedly call `resolveWorkflowRuntime` in local helper scope.

`main.go` follows the same pattern with runtime wrappers (`bootstrapRuntimeWith`, `runBootstrapSubsystems`) that re-resolve/validate callback carriers rather than operating on a fully-resolved dependency object through the call chain.
detail.suggestion: Resolve runtime/dependencies once at entrypoints (HTTP handler, startup task, workflow dispatcher) and pass a concrete resolved dependency object through downstream calls; remove variadic runtime parameters from inner helpers to make dependency contracts explicit.

review::.::holistic::abstraction_fitness.summary: Event publishing abstraction mixes global singleton and injected broker semantics

evidence: `events` package exposes mutable process-wide default runtime (`DefaultEventRuntime`, `SetDefaultEventRuntime`).
`workflowRuntime` carries an `EventRuntime` field but transition publishing actually goes through a separate `TransitionEmitter` closure captured from `DefaultEventRuntime()` at construction time.
Importer default runtime binds `PublishImportTransitionFn` directly to `events.DefaultEventRuntime().PublishImportShipTransition`, bypassing explicit broker ownership per operation context.
detail.suggestion: Pick one ownership model for event publishing: either fully injected broker per runtime (preferred) or centralized singleton. If injected, derive `TransitionEmitter` from the injected broker and remove direct `DefaultEventRuntime()` usage from runtime constructors.

review::.::holistic::cross_module_archit.summary: Owner contract metadata is governed by inconsistent types across catalogs.

evidence: `action/specs.go` introduces `type OwnerModule string` and uses typed constants (`OwnerUploadService`, `OwnerSystemWiFi`) before casting to string.
`startram/specs.go` uses a raw string constant (`OwnerStartram`) directly for the same `Owner` field.
This inconsistency means cross-family ownership policy is not enforced uniformly at compile time.
detail.suggestion: Create a shared owner-domain type and canonical owner constant set in a common contracts package (e.g., `catalog/common`), then make both action and startram specs consume that shared type to enforce one ownership contract path.

review::.::holistic::test_strategy::miss.summary: Conformance tests leave non-action contract families outside the quality gate.

evidence: StarTram error contracts are declared in `catalog/startram/specs.go` (`ContractSpecs()`), but there is no corresponding test in `conformance/fixtures_test.go` asserting StarTram catalog integrity.
Current conformance tests assert action fixture coverage and upload payload-rule governance only.
README backend quality-gate text frames contract conformance as a shared governance gate, but reviewed tests do not provide parallel coverage for non-action families.
detail.suggestion: Add conformance tests that iterate every contract family catalog (including StarTram) and assert canonical uniqueness/ID format/owner/message invariants, then include these tests in the same contract-gate command path so quality gates cover all governed families.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::s.summary: Global runtime singleton control is mixed with domain behavior in the same modules.

evidence: goseg/docker/events/events.go defines mutable global default runtime state (defaultEventRuntime + mutex + Default/Set/Reset) alongside EventRuntime publish/subscribe methods.
goseg/broadcast/state.go defines mutable global default state runtime (defaultBroadcastStateRuntime + mutex + Default/Set/Reset) alongside broadcast state mutation/collection/broadcast APIs.
goseg/importer/importer_test.go reads from events.DefaultEventRuntime() directly, showing tests depend on global singleton behavior rather than explicit broker injection.
detail.suggestion: Split singleton lifecycle management into dedicated provider modules (for example, runtime_provider.go) and keep EventRuntime/broadcast state types focused on behavior. Move call sites to injected runtime instances first, then keep global defaults only as narrow compatibility shims.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::r.summary: Runtime dependency composition is spread across many manual mapping points.

evidence: Runtime/StartupRuntime wiring requires synchronized edits across struct fields, default builders, merge functions, and option constructors (With*, default*, merge*, New*).
runtime_ops.go separately builds seam bundles and multiple default op groups, creating parallel composition flows for the same runtime graph.
WithSnapshotOps in runtime_core.go mutates RuntimeHealthOps as a side-effect of snapshot injection, which hides ownership boundaries between operation groups.
detail.suggestion: Centralize runtime composition in a single dependency registry/builder per runtime type, with one authoritative map of op groups and explicit post-processing hooks. Reduce hand-maintained mirrored wiring points.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::c.summary: Config patch schema file bundles multiple concerns that should be isolated.

evidence: goseg/config/config_update_schema.go contains patch structs, generic parser abstractions, patch-field registry init/validation, value parsers, and build/merge helpers in one file.
goseg/config/config_test.go includes very large fixture-heavy merge tests, signaling high cognitive load and broad behavior surface tied to the same schema module.
goseg/structs/configs.go adds additional config section mutation and persistence-shape logic, creating cross-file blending of schema, mutation, and serialization responsibilities.
detail.suggestion: Split config patch handling by concern: section patch models, parser/validator utilities, registry declaration, and merge/apply logic in separate files (or per-section packages). Keep SysConfig serialization/mutation helpers separate from patch parsing policies.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::t.summary: Several large test files aggregate unrelated behaviors instead of focused slices.

evidence: goseg/accesspoint/accesspoint_test.go (~963 LOC) mixes password resolution, config generation, process matching, shell execution, router startup, and teardown scenarios.
goseg/importer/importer_test.go (~1016 LOC) mixes transition publishing, request validation, chunk persistence, endpoint session semantics, and post-import workflow behavior.
goseg/system/storage/storage_test.go (~650 LOC) mixes SMART snapshots, mount detection, fstab parsing/writing, filesystem formatting, and path resolution.
goseg/main_test.go and goseg/protocol/actions/actions_test.go are also high-count multi-scenario files, reinforcing a repeated packaging pattern.
detail.suggestion: Split each large test file into behavior-focused test files (for example, *_runtime_test.go, *_parsing_test.go, *_workflow_test.go) and move shared setup/builders into local test helpers to reduce cross-scenario coupling.

review::.::holistic::error_consistency::.summary: Broadcast runtime precondition failures use incompatible error identities, making failure handling non-uniform.

evidence: `goseg/broadcast/loop.go` defines sentinel `errBroadcastLoopRuntimeRequired` but `runBroadcastTickWithRuntime`/`buildBroadcastTickState` return new string errors (`fmt.Errorf("broadcast runtime is required")`, `fmt.Errorf("broadcast state runtime is required")`).
`goseg/broadcast/state_lifecycle.go` uses another independent constructor (`errors.New("broadcast state runtime is required to reload urbits")`) for the same class of nil-runtime failure.
`goseg/broadcast/state.go` already centralizes similar runtime requirements via `errBroadcastRuntimeRequired`, showing competing conventions inside one subsystem.
detail.suggestion: Standardize runtime precondition errors around shared sentinels per subsystem and always wrap with `%w` when adding context. Replace string-only constructors for equivalent conditions so callers can reliably use `errors.Is` across broadcast entry points.

review::.::holistic::error_consistency::.summary: Long-running subsystem loops treat repeated operational failures inconsistently, producing unpredictable recovery behavior.

evidence: `dockerSubscriptionHandlerWithRuntime` tracks consecutive failures and returns a terminal error after `dockerTransitionFailureLimit`.
`check502Loop`/`processCheck502Pier` repeatedly log errors (`LoadUrbitConfigFn`, network lookup, remote polling, recovery failure) and continue without threshold-based escalation.
`DockerLogStreamerWithRuntime` and `RunOldLogsCleanupLoopWithContext` implement explicit consecutive-failure cutoffs, indicating another error policy in similar daemon-style loops.
detail.suggestion: Define a shared background-loop error policy (retry budget, backoff, and terminal criteria) and apply it consistently to docker event handling, 502 health checks, and logstream workers. Use one helper that records consecutive failure windows and returns typed terminal errors when policy is exceeded.

review::.::holistic::error_consistency::.summary: Importer workflow cleanup/publish failures can overshadow or silently drop the primary import failure context.

evidence: In `runImportPhases`, when workflow execution fails, later publish failures can become the returned error (`import phase transition publish failed ...`) instead of preserving the original workflow error as primary context.
`errorCleanup` returns rollback errors, but transition publish failures are only logged (`failed to publish ...`) and not propagated, which can hide notification-plane failures from callers.

Event publishing in `goseg/docker/events/events.go` has typed errors (`ErrTransitionBusFull`, `ErrTransitionPublishTimeout`), but importer paths do not consistently retain these typed causes end-to-end.
detail.suggestion: Return joined errors that always include the original workflow failure first, then cleanup/publish failures as secondary causes. For cleanup transition publication, propagate joined publish errors (or attach them to the returned cleanup error) instead of logging-only paths.

review::.::holistic::cross_module_archit.summary: Cross-module behavior depends on unsynchronized mutable singletons instead of explicit runtime wiring.

evidence: collectors_facade.go stores default collectors in package vars and exposes SetBroadcast* mutators that rewrite them globally.
state.go fetches collectors through DefaultBroadcast*Contract() in runtime paths (collect* methods), so global replacement can alter behavior across running code paths.
click.go uses a package-global runtimeRuntime with SetRuntime, introducing process-wide behavior mutation rather than constructor-based dependency ownership.
detail.suggestion: Replace mutable package-level defaults with constructor-injected dependencies: create explicit Runtime structs for broadcast/click, pass collector/runtime dependencies at bootstrap, and keep default globals only as immutable fallbacks (or guard mutable swaps with clear test-only lifecycle controls and synchronization).

review::.::holistic::error_consistency::.summary: Restore path flattens multi-step failures into strings while backup preserves typed error chains.

evidence: backup/service.go collects errors as []error and returns errors.Join(errs...), each component wrapped with `%w`.
restore/restore.go collects []string with `%v` and returns one formatted error string, losing wrapped component errors.
click.go exposes both as sibling API surface (BackupTlon/RestoreTlon), so callers get inconsistent diagnostics and recoverability behavior.
detail.suggestion: Unify backup/restore aggregation semantics by switching RestoreTlon to []error + errors.Join, preserving `%w` wrapping per component and documenting the shared multi-error contract in click package entrypoints.

review::.::holistic::abstraction_fitness.summary: Large wrapper interfaces add indirection cost with little behavior gain.

evidence: click.go declares a broad ClickRuntime and clickRuntime func-field struct; many methods are direct one-line forwards with no policy translation.
auth.go largely re-exports/forwards lifecycle and token-policy functions, adding another layer without strong boundary reduction.
The wrapper pattern repeats around click runtime command execution seams, increasing call depth and cognitive load for routine flows.
detail.suggestion: Collapse pass-through layers: keep abstraction only where policy translation exists (validation, mapping, retry, auth context), and move simple forwarding to direct package calls or narrower interfaces per capability (e.g., desk, token, backup/restore) to reduce indirection surface.

review::.::holistic::test_strategy::non_.summary: Background lifecycle loops lack cancellation hooks, limiting deterministic tests.

evidence: DockerPoller in poller.go runs forever on ticker events with no context or stop signal argument.
Runtime in lifecycle.go carries poll interval and tick function seams but no lifecycle stop primitive for DockerPoller itself.
bootstrap.go uses sync.Once and package globals for initialization state, making full lifecycle restart paths hard to exercise in a single test process.
detail.suggestion: Add context-aware lifecycle entrypoints (e.g., DockerPoller(ctx)) and expose explicit reset/test lifecycle hooks for global startup state behind a controlled interface so integration tests can start/stop subsystems deterministically.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::o.summary: Core orchestration functions combine unrelated concerns, obscuring intent and safe change boundaries.

evidence: initializeConfig in bootstrap.go handles environment/context setup, disk I/O, migration/merge, persistence, key management, and readiness signaling in one body.
startRouterWithRuntime in router.go mixes preflight, interface setup, iptables policy, service launch, and timing coordination; stopRouterWithRuntime similarly bundles teardown concerns.
config_update_executor.go couples patch application and persistence under global lock, reinforcing large all-in-one workflows rather than smaller composable operations.
detail.suggestion: Refactor orchestration into explicit phases with focused helpers (preflight, mutate-in-memory model, persist/apply side effects, post-commit signaling), and model each phase with clear inputs/outputs so failures and retries are localized.

review::.::holistic::high_level_elegance.summary: Action catalog combines protocol registry and upload domain policy, blurring ownership boundaries.

evidence: `ProtocolActionSpecs()` and `UploadActionSpecs()` coexist in the same catalog module even though one is generic protocol registry and the other encodes upload-specific payload policy.
`UploadActionFamilySpecs()` exists only to project upload-specific structs back into generic action specs, showing a leaky boundary between domain policy and protocol catalog.

Conformance and uploadsvc tests both need to assert canonical upload/C2C contract alignment, which suggests contract ownership is spread across package seams rather than centered behind one authoritative interface.
detail.suggestion: Split `catalog/action` into explicit ownership layers: keep a pure protocol action registry in one package/file, move upload payload-policy specs to an upload-domain contract package, and expose one canonical read API (used by both conformance and uploadsvc tests) so cross-package matrix validation can be reduced to validating that single seam.

review::.::holistic::mid_level_elegance:.summary: Runtime constructors capture EventRuntime snapshots by value, so broker swaps do not propagate across seams.

evidence: DefaultEventRuntime() returns EventRuntime by value in goseg/docker/events/events.go.
newWorkflowRuntime() stores defaultEventRuntime := events.DefaultEventRuntime() and emits via that captured value.
defaultImporterRuntime() wires PublishImportTransitionFn from events.DefaultEventRuntime().PublishImportShipTransition, also snapshot-based.
SetDefaultEventRuntime replaces the global runtime later, but already-constructed runtimes continue publishing on previously captured channels.
detail.suggestion: Return and pass an EventBroker reference (pointer/interface) from a single composition root, and inject it into workflow/importer constructors so runtime swaps are explicit and consistent.

review::.::holistic::dependency_health::.summary: Multiple package-level default runtimes/controllers create hidden mutable dependencies and split-brain behavior risks.

evidence: broadcast has mutable global defaults (DefaultBroadcastStateRuntime/SetDefaultBroadcastStateRuntime).
broadcast loop uses a separate mutable default controller (defaultBroadcastLoopController).
events package has an independent mutable default runtime (defaultEventRuntime).
docker subsystem has its own default runtime instance (defaultDockerSubsystemRuntime).
These defaults are independently replaceable and can diverge across modules, increasing hidden coupling and test/runtime order sensitivity.
detail.suggestion: Introduce a single top-level runtime container built at startup and pass explicit dependencies into subsystem constructors; keep package-level defaults only as thin legacy adapters.

review::.::holistic::mid_level_elegance:.summary: Very wide callback bags degrade seam clarity and shift integration failures from compile time to runtime.

evidence: RuntimeContainerOps/RuntimeSnapshotOps/RuntimeUrbitOps aggregate many unrelated callbacks in goseg/docker/orchestration/runtime_ops.go.
importerRuntime embeds multiple large runtime groups and relies on runtime string-tag validation plus many missing-dependency error sentinels.
workflowRuntime embeds several orchestration runtime bags directly, mixing lifecycle, eventing, config snapshots, and container control at one seam.
startup orchestrator uses name-keyed service callback maps that further widen and loosen integration boundaries.
detail.suggestion: Refactor runtimes into smaller capability interfaces per use-case (e.g., transition publisher, container lifecycle, urbit config mutation), and inject only required capabilities per flow to reduce nil-runtime and wiring drift.

review::.::holistic::low_level_elegance:.summary: Fail-fast health checking spawns goroutines that can block indefinitely on service error channels.

evidence: waitForFailFastErrors creates one goroutine per fail-fast service that does a blocking receive from monitored.err.
For healthy long-running services, monitored.err does not close until shutdown, so those goroutines remain blocked.
The receive goroutines are not canceled via timeout context, only the caller select returns on timeout.
detail.suggestion: Remove per-call goroutines and use non-blocking/timeout-aware selects directly on service channels, or track a one-time watcher per service with lifecycle tied to startup context cancellation.

review::.::holistic::package_organizatio.summary: Large flat directories obscure ownership and make change paths unpredictable.

evidence: Directory profiles show config (41 files), docker/orchestration (34), and shipworkflow (55), each above the >10 mixed-concern split threshold in this review rubric.
Reviewed files from these directories represent different layers (bootstrap/runtime context/domain workflow/container orchestration) co-located without stronger folder boundaries.
detail.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: (1) create focused subfolders `goseg/config/bootstrap/`, `goseg/shipworkflow/newship/`, `goseg/docker/orchestration/containerbridge/`; (2) move files in order with `git mv goseg/config/bootstrap.go goseg/config/bootstrap/bootstrap.go`, `git mv goseg/shipworkflow/newship.go goseg/shipworkflow/newship/handler.go`, `git mv goseg/shipworkflow/newship_service.go goseg/shipworkflow/newship/runtime.go`, `git mv goseg/docker/orchestration/container_bridge.go goseg/docker/orchestration/containerbridge/runtime_bridge.go`, `git mv goseg/docker/orchestration/container_config.go goseg/docker/orchestration/containerbridge/config_builder.go`; (3) update package names/imports with `goimports -w` (or `gofmt` plus manual import edits); (4) validate with `go test ./goseg/config/... ./goseg/shipworkflow/... ./goseg/docker/orchestration/...` and `go test ./...`.

review::.::holistic::package_organizatio.summary: Container adapter/build logic is misplaced in orchestration root instead of container-focused boundary package.

evidence: container_bridge.go is a runtime-input projection and adapter layer for Llama/Netdata/Minio container runtimes rather than general orchestration entry behavior.
container_config.go is type-to-builder dispatch for container-specific config constructors, but it lives alongside broad orchestration runtime files in the same root directory.
detail.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: (1) create `goseg/docker/orchestration/containerbridge/`; (2) move bridge and config dispatcher first, then tests (`git mv .../container_bridge.go .../containerbridge/runtime_bridge.go`, `git mv .../container_config.go .../containerbridge/config_builder.go`, `git mv .../container_bridge_test.go .../containerbridge/runtime_bridge_test.go`); (3) keep a thin compatibility shim in old package temporarily that delegates to new package; (4) update call sites via `rg -n "ContainerConfigForType|llamaRuntimeFromDocker|netdataRuntimeFromDocker|minioRuntimeFromDocker" goseg`; (5) validate with `go test ./goseg/docker/orchestration/...`.

review::.::holistic::high_level_elegance.summary: New-ship flow blends transport parsing, domain policy, and infra wiring in top-level workflow files.

evidence: HandleNewShip in newship.go parses websocket payload actions and also coordinates cancel/reset behavior directly, while HandleNewShipBoot performs validation, drive prep, event publication, and async provisioning launch.

newship_service.go constructs provisioning runtime by directly binding config, docker/network orchestration, and startram operations, so boundary ownership between workflow orchestration and infrastructure composition is unclear.
detail.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: (1) split `newship.go` into `newship/transport_handler.go` (action decode/dispatch), `newship/validator.go` (patp/key/drive validation), and `newship/usecase.go` (orchestration flow); (2) move infrastructure binding from `newship_service.go` into `newship/runtimebinding/` with explicit interfaces consumed by usecase layer; (3) update imports and exported entrypoints (`HandleNewShip`, `HandleNewShipBoot`) to thin façade functions; (4) run `go test ./goseg/shipworkflow/...` and then `go test ./...`.

review::.::holistic::high_level_elegance.summary: Contract governance is fragmented across docs, catalog specs, and tests without one authoritative code surface.

evidence: README.md declares governance and quality-gate contract expectations, but runnable enforcement shown here is spread across two separate test packages.
Action and StarTram contract families use different local composition patterns and do not expose a unified governance registry from the reviewed files.
Package documentation points to policy docs, but change safety still depends on engineers discovering multiple files across protocol/contracts and uploadsvc.
detail.suggestion: Create a single governance entrypoint in protocol/contracts that exports all family specs plus required conformance checks, then have README.md and uploadsvc/doc.go reference that entrypoint directly (not only prose docs). Keep tests focused on verifying the entrypoint output rather than reconstructing policy in multiple places.

review::.::holistic::package_organizatio.summary: Contract policy placement is scattered across catalog, conformance, uploadsvc tests, and root docs.

evidence: Governance-relevant files for one concern are distributed across four directory areas: root docs, protocol/contracts/catalog, protocol/contracts/conformance, and uploadsvc.
directory_profiles show separate small folders for action, startram, and conformance, but no reviewed in-tree index file ties them into a navigable contract-policy package boundary.
uploadsvc test coverage doubles as cross-package governance validation, which blurs folder ownership between runtime behavior tests and contract-governance conformance.
detail.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: (1) add `goseg/protocol/contracts/governance/` with `registry.go` and `conformance_policy.go` that aggregate family specs and required payload-rule assertions; (2) move `catalog/action/specs.go` and `catalog/startram/specs.go` behind the governance registry API while keeping family files as leaf declarations; (3) relocate cross-package governance assertions from `goseg/uploadsvc/actions_test.go` into `goseg/protocol/contracts/conformance/` and keep uploadsvc tests runtime-local. After each stage run `cd goseg && go test ./protocol/contracts/...`, then `cd goseg && go test ./uploadsvc/...`, then `cd goseg && go test ./...` to validate imports and behavior.

review::.::holistic::initialization_coup.summary: Core services rely on mutable package-level singletons and init order, creating hidden boot/test coupling.

evidence: Config/bootstrap gates state through `initOnce` plus globals (`Ready`, `globalConfig`, `GSContainers`).
Importer request path depends on global `uploadDir`/`tempDir`/`uploadSessions` populated by prior `Initialize` call.
Broadcast/events each expose mutable default runtimes via `SetDefault...`, so behavior can vary by prior caller mutation.
detail.suggestion: Introduce explicit application-scoped runtime structs (config state, importer session store, event broker, broadcast state) and pass them through startup wiring; keep package defaults only as thin compatibility adapters.

review::.::holistic::type_safety::urbit_.summary: Urbit section update seams erase compile-time types with `any`, allowing invalid mutator/section pairings until runtime.

evidence: Runtime contract declares `UpdateUrbitSectionFn func(patp string, section UrbitConfigSection, mutateFn func(any) error) error`.
`persistShipUrbitSection` forwards a `func(any) error`, so section-target compatibility is not enforced by the type system.
detail.suggestion: Replace the untyped section mutator seam with typed per-section callbacks (or a generic typed registry keyed by section enum) so invalid section/mutator combinations fail at compile time.

review::.::holistic::contract_coherence:.summary: README contract guidance points to deprecated/incorrect action package paths, diverging from actual runtime contract modules.

evidence: README says canonical websocket action registry is `protocol/actions` and references `groundseg/protocol/actions.ActionC2CConnect`.
Current implementation/tests consume `groundseg/protocol/contracts` action bindings and catalog specs.
detail.suggestion: Update README contract section to the current `protocol/contracts` entrypoints and keep referenced constant paths synchronized with conformance tests.

review::.::holistic::naming_quality::wif.summary: Wi-Fi runtime naming alternates between `WiFi` and `Wifi`, reducing discoverability and grepability within one API family.

evidence: API file exports `WiFiRuntimeService`, `IsWiFiEnabled`, `StartWiFiInfoLoop`.
Runtime methods file defines fields like `NewWifiClient`/`WifiInfoTicker` and `wifiNmcli*` types, mixing acronym style in the same subsystem.
detail.suggestion: Normalize acronym casing to one convention (`WiFi` or `Wifi`) across the wifi runtime/service surface and update dependent call sites/tests together.

review::.::holistic::authorization_consi.summary: High-impact new-ship operations expose handler APIs without explicit authorization context while upload flows enforce token checks at boundary.

evidence: `validateUploadRequest` and session setup in importer require upload token IDs/hashes and authorization results.
`HandleNewShip` / `HandleNewShipBoot` / `CancelNewShip` accept payload/action input and trigger provisioning/deletion paths without auth token/session parameters at this API boundary.
detail.suggestion: Align boundary contracts by requiring an authenticated actor/session context for new-ship entrypoints (or enforce an explicit pre-authorized command type) and document that contract next to handler APIs.
